key_concerns: Barriers to international pediatric oncology trials
key_positives: FDA proactively seeking input to improve pediatric oncology development
Emphasis on international collaboration and clinical trial networks
Focus on identifying relevant molecular targets

evidence_notes: No clinical data presented; document only contains discussion questions.
Document states: 'Please discuss your perspectives on how the 2017 FDA Reauthorization Act (FDARA) is impacting pediatric oncology' – indicates FDA is evaluating legislative impact.

Document states: 'Please discuss factors that should be considered when determining whether nonclinical proof-of-concept studies should be conducted' – highlights lack of standardized preclinical evidence requirements.
Document states: 'Please discuss the role of pediatric clinical trial networks and international collaboration' – signals recognition of needed infrastructure improvements.

document.title: May 22, 2024 Meeting of the Pediatric Oncology Subcommittee of the Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee- Final Questions

signal.label: MIXED
rationale: Concerns: Challenges with FDARA implementation; Uncertainty in preclinical proof-of-concept requirements; Barriers to international pediatric oncology trials

Positives: FDA proactively seeking input to improve pediatric oncology development; Emphasis on international collaboration and clinical trial networks; Focus on identifying relevant molecular targets
